what_it_does: Acceptance test for the fabric native tool-calling path. It monkeypatches HTTP and sleep to verify that openai_tools_transport builds tools/tool_choice into the request and returns the full message dict, that complete_with_tools validates responses and retries correctly, and that model_supports_tools detects tool capability for Ollama and LiteLLM endpoints while failing loud when detection is impossible or Gemini is used.
observations: Uses injected fake transports and a monkeypatched urllib.request.urlopen so no live endpoints are required.
Defines reusable test helpers: check, recording_sleep, tools_transport_of, and FakeResp.
Verifies request body construction including tools, default tool_choice='auto', and explicit override.
Tests complete_with_tools guard: empty content with tool_calls is valid; empty content without tool_calls retries then raises FabricError; transport errors retry then succeed or fail.
Counts attempts and sleep calls to enforce exact retry/backoff behavior.
Tests model_supports_tools for Ollama /api/show capabilities and LiteLLM /model/info supports_function_calling.
Enforces forbid_gemini before any HTTP call in both transport and capability probe.
Treats undetectable capability as a raise, never silent True or False.
standouts: Fail-loud capability detection: unreachable, missing capabilities, unknown endpoint, or missing LiteLLM field all raise.
Deterministic fake-injected acceptance test that also serves as a design document.
Strict guard on complete_with_tools distinguishes empty content with tool_calls from empty content without tool_calls.
conventions: Acceptance test written as an executable design spec with a long explanatory docstring.
Monkeypatching of module-level HTTP client for headless testing.
Global boolean ok aggregated by a check helper.
Nested closures inside main for scenario-specific fakes.
concerns: Monkeypatching transport.urllib.request.urlopen globally could affect other tests if not restored; it is restored in finally blocks.
Relies on internal signatures of fabric.client.complete_with_tools, transport.openai_tools_transport, and transport.model_supports_tools that are not defined in this file.
References ':71 empty-fail' which is not explained within this file.
Global ok state makes the test order-dependent and harder to parallelize.
notes: A headless, fake-injected acceptance test that locks down the fabric native tool-calling lane, its retry/guard semantics, and fail-loud endpoint-aware capability detection.
questions: What is the ':71 empty-fail' mentioned in the docstring and success assertions?
Do fabric.client.complete_with_tools and transport.openai_tools_transport already exist with the exact signatures assumed here?
Is the endpoint parameter of model_supports_tools intended to accept arbitrary endpoint kinds beyond 'ollama' and 'litellm'?
Why does the test treat tool_calls=[] equivalently to no tool_calls for the empty-content retry case?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file tests exactly the three numbered behaviors described in its docstring.
apparent_themes: Sibling extension without modifying existing code
Guard and retry with precise backoff accounting
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish a new, strictly validated native tool-calling path in the fabric client, keeping it separate from existing transports, while enforcing loud failures for unsupported models and undetectable capabilities.
novelty: It encodes an acceptance contract as a runnable script that asserts not just outputs but the exact number of attempts and sleep calls, and it treats capability uncertainty as an explicit exception rather than a default boolean.
contribution: An executable specification that defines expected behavior for the native tool-calling transport, the complete_with_tools guard/retry contract, and endpoint-aware tool-capability detection, along with reusable fake helpers for headless validation.
summary_for_embedding: Headless acceptance test for the fabric native tool-calling path that verifies request body construction, message-dict return values, retry/guard behavior, and endpoint-aware tool-capability detection using monkeypatched HTTP fakes.
intention_for_embedding: To specify and validate a fail-loud, sibling native tool-calling lane without altering the existing complete/openai transport implementation.

symbols.does: Records a boolean test result and prints PASS/FAIL.
symbols.description: The check helper updates the global ok flag based on a condition and emits a labeled PASS/FAIL line to stdout, serving as the test harness's assertion primitive.

symbols.does: Returns a fake sleep function that records every delay it receives.
symbols.description: Produces a tuple of a call list and a closure that appends each sleep duration, enabling tests to assert exact backoff counts without actually waiting.

symbols.does: Builds a fake tools transport that yields a sequence of responses or exceptions.
symbols.description: Creates a closure transport and a seen metadata dict; each call consumes the next prepared response, records the passed opts, and either returns a message dict or raises, supporting retry and wiring assertions.

symbols.does: The fake transport closure returned by tools_transport_of.
symbols.description: Accepts model, messages, and keyword opts, advances the response iterator, stores opts for later inspection, and returns or raises the next prepared value.

symbols.does: Stand-in HTTP response context manager for urllib fakes.
symbols.description: Wraps a JSON payload so it can be returned from a patched urlopen, providing read and context-manager methods that mimic a minimal urllib response object.

symbols.does: Serializes the given payload to JSON bytes.
symbols.description: Stores the JSON-encoded payload so subsequent read calls can return it as response body bytes.

symbols.does: Returns the stored JSON payload bytes.
symbols.description: Provides the response body that patched urlopen callers expect when reading the fake HTTP response.

symbols.does: Returns self as the context-managed response.
symbols.description: Allows the fake response to be used in a with statement by yielding itself.

symbols.does: Exits the context manager without suppressing exceptions.
symbols.description: Implements the context-manager cleanup protocol and always returns False so any active exception propagates.

symbols.does: Runs all acceptance assertions for the fabric tools path.
symbols.description: Patches urllib.request.urlopen, exercises openai_tools_transport, complete_with_tools, and model_supports_tools with injected fakes, and prints a final pass/fail banner based on the aggregated ok flag.

symbols.does: Captures request data and returns a fake OpenAI-style chat response.
symbols.description: Nested inside the first test block, it records the posted JSON body and URL, then returns a FakeResp containing a choices[0].message with content and tool_calls.

symbols.does: Factory that builds a fake urlopen for Ollama /api/show or LiteLLM /model/info probes.
symbols.description: Returns a closure that either raises a provided exception or captures the request URL and body and returns a FakeResp carrying the supplied capability payload.

symbols.does: The fake urlopen closure produced by fake_show.
symbols.description: Handles a single HTTP request for capability probes, raising if configured or returning a JSON payload wrapped in FakeResp while recording the request for later assertions.
